title: Define release-state and PackageReleaseProof contract
rationale: Publication needs a structured proof boundary before any publish operation, or readiness will be confused with authority.
acceptance: States ready_to_publish, actually_published, and registry_discoverable are distinct
PackageReleaseProof includes packageShapeProof, accountNamespaceProof, publishOperationProof, provenanceProof, registryDiscoverabilityProof, runtimeSmokeProof, authorityBoundary, and proofGaps
authorityBoundary contains explicit false fields for non-authority claims

title: Remove engineering-agent-only public framing
rationale: The hard invariant is protected actions for automated decision making; engineering agents are a domain wedge, not the product center.
acceptance: README and package-facing docs use protected-actions framing
Engineering-agent language is limited to wedge/example status
x402 exact per-call is described as first proof wedge only
non_goals: Do not broaden into generic auth
Do not reposition x402 as protocol center

title: Harden non-authority publication wording
rationale: Package and registry metadata must not imply authority, policy decisions, greenlights, gateway checks, mutation, custody, trust, settlement, or hosted operation.
acceptance: server.json remains metadata-only
README states publication does not create authority
CLI/MCP descriptions avoid mutation or enforcement claims
non_goals: Do not add payment management claims

title: Extend package surface checks for authority-boundary claims
rationale: Existing package checks cover shape but not the full publication authority boundary.
acceptance: Pack dry-run remains mandatory
Forbidden path checks remain mandatory
Checks fail on authority-overclaiming metadata or public text
Checks preserve no raw PaymentPayload or SIGNATURE leakage
non_goals: Do not inspect private npm account state in source

title: Create release manifest preflight requirements
rationale: A publish operation without an exact manifest is not reconstructable six months later.
acceptance: Manifest records package name, version, tag, pack evidence, exports, bins, account posture, provenance posture, authority boundary, stop conditions, and proof gaps
Manifest separates local readiness from publish evidence
Manifest blocks unknown credential posture

title: Verify npm account, 2FA, token, and provenance posture
rationale: Credential posture is part of publication risk; silent fallback to risky tokens is unacceptable.
acceptance: OIDC/trusted publishing availability is recorded
2FA/token posture is recorded
Bypass-2FA token usage is blocked or explicitly recorded as rejected risk
Provenance support is recorded or proof gap is named
non_goals: Do not treat provenance as runtime enforcement

title: Publish exact npm package version only after preflight passes
rationale: Publication mutates public artifact state and must be tied to exact package/version/provenance evidence.
acceptance: Publish uses exact package version and tag
Publish operation evidence is captured
Failure does not advance actually_published
Provenance evidence or proof gap is recorded
non_goals: Do not submit MCP Registry metadata yet

title: Run clean install smoke against public npm artifact
rationale: Local source success does not prove public installability or installed runtime behavior.
acceptance: Clean install by package name and version succeeds
Installed handshake bin runs
Installed handshake-mcp bin runs
Installed package-name bin runs
Installed exports resolve
Installed messaging remains non-authority
non_goals: Do not rely on local checkout
Do not infer downstream business success

title: Write post-publish verification receipt
rationale: The receipt must distinguish package shape, publish operation, provenance, install smoke, and proof gaps.
acceptance: Receipt marks actually_published only after clean install passes
Receipt records proof gaps separately
Receipt preserves authorityBoundary false fields
Receipt is reconstructable from source-owned evidence
non_goals: Do not erase failed publish attempts
Do not merge registry evidence into npm evidence

title: Verify MCP Registry metadata and namespace posture
rationale: MCP Registry is metadata/discoverability only and requires namespace/package-name alignment.
acceptance: server.json uses public install methods
package.json mcpName matches server.json name
Namespace auth evidence exists or blocks
Registry wording stays metadata-only
Preview limitations are recorded
non_goals: Do not host artifacts in registry

title: Submit MCP Registry metadata after npm proof passes
rationale: Registry submission before npm artifact verification would create discoverability for an unproven package.
acceptance: Registry submission evidence is captured
Failure does not advance registry_discoverable
Registry operation evidence is separate from npm publish evidence

title: Verify registry discoverability and finalize release support bundle
rationale: Discoverability must be evidenced and packaged with explicit readiness statuses and proof gaps.
acceptance: Registry listing is discoverable
Listing install instructions match source-owned metadata
Support bundle uses allowed readiness statuses
registry_discoverable is set only after verification
MCP preview instability is recorded if present
non_goals: Do not claim payment or settlement management
